title: Ephemerantha kunstleri
intro: Ephemerantha kunstleri, also known as Kunstler's orchid, is a species of orchid native to Borneo. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, making it a potential treatment for various ailments such as wound healing and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Ephemerantha kunstleri include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and it is also used in modern herbal preparations such as capsules, tinctures, and extracts.
taxonomy: Ephemerantha kunstleri is a species of orchid. The genus Ephemerantha is a part of the orchid family (Orchidaceae) and subfamily Vanilloideae. Ephemerantha kunstleri is further classified under the tribe Vanillinae. This classification indicates the plant's evolutionary relationships within the orchid family. Ephemerantha kunstleri's classification also highlights its unique characteristics and shared traits with other members of the Vanilloideae subfamily.
